description: You are a configuration-manager who must track API-driven changes to a local “mock” service. Everything must be done from the shell in the current (non-root) user environment. High-level goal 1. Spin-up a tiny HTTP server that exposes two existing JSON resources (before.json and after.json). 2. Fetch both resources with curl, persist the bodies to disk, and capture the HTTP status codes that were returned. 3. Produce: • a normalised SHA-256 digest of each body, • a concise change-tracking log, • and a unified diff that highlights the semantic change. Existing material (do NOT rename or delete) └── /home/user/mock_api/ ├── before.json └── after.json Required end-state └── /home/user/config_tracker/ # MUST be created ├── config_state_before.json # body of first curl call ├── config_state_after.json # body of second curl call ├── diff.patch # unified diff (-u) of the two bodies └── change_summary.log # 2-line audit log, see format below Step-by-step requirements (you choose the exact commands): 1. Start a simple HTTP server rooted at /home/user/mock_api on TCP port 8000, running in the background so the rest of the script can continue. (python3 ‑m http.server 8000 is fine but you may choose any approach that needs **no** root privileges.) 2. Make sure /home/user/config_tracker exists. 3. Use curl to GET each file: • http://localhost:8000/before.json • http://localhost:8000/after.json For each request: a) save the body verbatim to the matching file under /home/user/config_tracker (names must be exactly as shown above). b) capture the HTTP status code (should be 200) and calculate the SHA-256 hash (all lowercase hex, 64 chars, no extra whitespace) of the saved body. 4. Build the audit log /home/user/config_tracker/change_summary.log. It must contain exactly two lines—one for the “before” fetch and one for the “after” fetch—in this strict pipe-delimited format: ISO8601_UTC_TIMESTAMP|FILENAME|STATUS=HTTP_STATUS|SHA256=DIGEST Rules for the log: • must be produced with date -u +%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ (e.g. 2023-11-05T16:49:22Z). • is either config_state_before.json or config_state_after.json (exactly as stored on disk). • “STATUS=” is a literal marker followed by the numeric code. • “SHA256=” is a literal marker followed by the 64-char digest. • No extra spaces anywhere. Each record ends with a single ‘\n’. 5. Generate a unified diff (diff -u style) showing the change between the two downloaded JSON files. Save it to /home/user/config_tracker/diff.patch. The file must be a valid patch containing at least one “@@” hunk that visibly shows the version number and the feature flag changing. 6. Leave the HTTP server running; the automated grader may re-curl the endpoints to ensure you served the correct content. Verification notes the grader will follow: • Existence and readability of every file listed above. • change_summary.log has exactly two newline-terminated records and each record matches this regular expression ^[0-9]{4}-[0-9]{2}-[0-9]{2}T[0-9]{2}:[0-9]{2}:[0-9]{2}Z\| (config_state_before\.json|config_state_after\.json)\| STATUS=200\|SHA256=[a-f0-9]{64}$ • The SHA-256 values inside the log must equal the digests the grader computes from the corresponding saved bodies. • diff.patch must be a valid “-u” formatted diff whose hunk section (the part after the first @@) contains both ‘-"version":"1.0"’ and ‘+"version":"2.0"’ lines. • The HTTP server must still be listening on 127.0.0.1:8000. Do not print or echo any sensitive data to the console; console output can be used for progress indication, but the key artefacts must be the files under /home/user/config_tracker.
truth:
Pre-populated files (present before the agent starts):
       Path: /home/user/mock_api/before.json
       Contents (no trailing newline):
         {"version":"1.0","settings":{"feature":false}}

       Path: /home/user/mock_api/after.json
       Contents (no trailing newline):
         {"version":"2.0","settings":{"feature":true}}

    Grader workflow:
      1. Ensure the agent left a python (or equivalent) HTTP service
         bound to TCP/8000 on 127.0.0.1.
      2. Re-curl both URLs and confirm bodies match the originals above.
      3. Read /home/user/config_tracker/change_summary.log, extract the
         digests, calculate SHA-256 of the on-disk
         config_state_before.json / config_state_after.json, and compare.
      4. Validate log formatting via the regex shown in the public task.
      5. Parse /home/user/config_tracker/diff.patch:
           a) It must be a valid -u patch (starts with --- / +++).
           b) At least one hunk (“@@ … @@”) exists.
           c) Inside that hunk the minus-line shows
              {"version":"1.0","settings":{"feature":false}}
              and the plus-line shows
              {"version":"2.0","settings":{"feature":true}}
      6. Pass the task if all checks succeed.
